info.title: BankSync API
info.description: Connect bank accounts and sync financial data to Notion, Google Sheets, and Airtable. Authenticate with an API key (X-API-Key header) or a Firebase ID token (Bearer).

apiKey.name: X-API-Key
apiKey.description: API key — pass in the X-API-Key header. Obtain from Developer Settings in the BankSync app.

bearerAuth.description: Firebase ID token — pass as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

startDate.description: Start date (YYYY-MM-DD) for date-range syncs (transactions, trades).

endDate.description: End date (YYYY-MM-DD) for date-range syncs (transactions, trades).

forceCancelActive.description: When true, cancel any currently-running sync on this feed before starting the new one. Use this for user-initiated retries when the previous sync is stuck. Defaults to false.

resetCursors.description: When true, clear all per-bank cursors, tier-1/tier-2 dedup state, and the date-based watermark before fetching. Use for backfills or recovery when the incremental cursor has gone stale. Defaults to false (normal incremental sync).

skipDedup.description: When true, bypass the per-record dedup guard for this run. Use for backfills where upstream IDs are known-stable but downstream dedup has drifted. Defaults to false.

get.description: List banks owned by the current workspace. Pass `?scope=family` to include banks from every portal under this workspace — useful for accountants who want a single view across all client portals. Family scope requires admin+ role and is only valid on a top-level workspace.

schema.description: Resource scope. `self` (default) returns this workspace only. `family` returns this workspace plus every live portal under it; each row gains a `portal: { id, name, icon } | null` badge and a `workspaceId` field. Admin+ only; top-level workspaces only.

parameters.description: Resource scope. `self` (default) returns this workspace only. `family` returns this workspace plus every live portal under it; each row gains a `portal: { id, name, icon } | null` badge and a `workspaceId` field. Admin+ only; top-level workspaces only.
parameters.name: scope

200.description: List of banks. When `scope=family`, each row additionally carries `workspaceId` and `portal`.

400.description: Invalid `scope` value, or `scope=family` was requested against a portal workspace

post.description: Initiates a provider-specific connection flow. Returns a `ConnectionStart` payload the client renders — a Plaid Link token, a portal popup URL, or an OAuth redirect URL depending on the provider.

501.description: Provider not available in this environment

post.description: Exchanges the provider callback payload (public_token or connection_id) for a persisted bank connection. Returns the bank ID, whether it was newly created, and live account data.

501.description: Provider not available in this environment

post.description: Produces a new connection start payload for an existing bank. Use when the connection has expired or been revoked.

Search the institution catalog for providers currently connectable in this workspace and environment. Results exclude providers whose feature gate is disabled as well as catalog-only seed data that cannot be connected in-app.

schema.description: Cursor for incremental delta sync (providers that support delta-cursor mode)

parameters.description: Cursor for incremental delta sync (providers that support delta-cursor mode)
parameters.name: cursor

get.summary: Get live balance for an account
List investment trades for an account
List current investment holdings for an account
List open and pending orders for an account
get.description: Returns orders in PENDING/PARTIALLY_FILLED/ACCEPTED/QUEUED/TRIGGERED status. Executed orders surface through the /trades endpoint instead. Returns an empty list for providers that do not expose orders (Plaid, SaltEdge).

get.summary: Get loan or liability details for an account

200.description: Loan / liability details for the account

get.description: List feeds owned by the current workspace. Pass `?scope=family` to include feeds from every portal under this workspace. Family scope requires admin+ role and is only valid on a top-level workspace.

schema.description: Resource scope. `self` (default) returns this workspace only. `family` includes every portal under it; each row gains `workspaceId` + `portal: { id, name, icon } | null`. Admin+ only, top-level workspaces only.

parameters.description: Resource scope. `self` (default) returns this workspace only. `family` includes every portal under it; each row gains `workspaceId` + `portal: { id, name, icon } | null`. Admin+ only, top-level workspaces only.
parameters.name: scope

200.description: List of feeds. When `scope=family`, each row additionally carries `workspaceId` and `portal`.

400.description: Invalid `scope` value, or `scope=family` was requested against a portal workspace

name.description: Human-readable feed name, e.g. "ANZ Transactions → Notion"

source.description: "sync" = bank API; "extractor" = email/PDF AI extraction; "upload" = file upload

dataType.description: Type of financial data this feed produces

accounts.description: One or more bank accounts to sync from

oneOf.description: Bank API sync source — pulls structured financial data from Plaid, SaltEdge, or SnapTrade

inboxAddress.description: Auto-generated forwarding email address (set by system on creation)

whitelistEnabled.description: When true, only emails from addresses in the whitelist are processed

whitelist.description: Allowed sender email addresses (only used when whitelistEnabled is true)

oneOf.description: Email extraction source — processes forwarded emails using AI
File upload source — processes uploaded PDFs/images using AI

sourceConfig.description: How to connect to the data source. Required before triggering a sync.

integrationId.description: Notion integration ID — get from list_integrations

databaseId.description: Notion database ID to write records into

integrationId.description: Airtable integration ID — get from list_integrations

tableId.description: Airtable table ID to write records into

integrationId.description: Google Sheets integration ID — get from list_integrations

sheetName.description: Name of the sheet tab to write into

direction.description: "row" = each record is a row (default); "column" = each record is a column

headerRowOrColumn.description: Row number (for row direction) or column number (for column direction) that contains headers

pushLocation.description: Where to insert new records: "beginning" prepends, "end" appends

attachmentFolderId.description: Optional Google Drive folder ID for storing file attachments

oneOf.description: Write to a Google Sheets spreadsheet

integrationId.description: Excel integration ID — get from list_integrations

workbookId.description: Excel workbook item ID (get from searchDatabases)

worksheetName.description: Name of the worksheet tab to write into

direction.description: "row" = each record is a row (default); "column" = each record is a column

headerRowOrColumn.description: Row number (for row direction) or column number (for column direction) that contains headers

pushLocation.description: Where to insert new records: "beginning" prepends, "end" appends

attachmentFolderId.description: Optional OneDrive folder ID for storing file attachments

attachmentDriveId.description: Drive ID for the attachment folder (defaults to workbook driveId)

preferTablesApi.description: Prefer Excel Tables API when target is inside an Excel Table (org accounts only)

oneOf.description: Write to a Microsoft Excel workbook on OneDrive

integrationId.description: Webhook endpoint integration ID — get from list_integrations

oneOf.description: POST signed JSON to a customer HTTPS endpoint. No field mapping: the payload is the standard versioned envelope (see the webhooks developer docs). Supports transactions, balances, holdings, trades, orders, and loans.

destinationConfig.description: Where to write the data. Required before triggering a sync.

sourceField.description: Source field key — get valid keys from get_data_type_schema

destinationField.description: Destination property ID (Notion/Airtable) or column name (Sheets)

fieldMappings.description: Maps source fields to destination columns/properties

pendingMode.description: "sync_all" — include pending transactions as-is; "filter_pending" — exclude pending transactions; "track_and_update" — sync pending and update records when they settle (recommended)

mode.description: "append" — add a new row for each sync (historical log); "overwrite" — replace the existing record (live snapshot)

examples.description: Example values to improve extraction accuracy

isItemField.description: True if this field is part of a line-item list (e.g. individual receipt items)

extractionFields.description: Fields the AI will extract from each document

itemizePerRow.description: True = one destination row per extracted item; False = one row per document

extractionInstructions.description: Custom instructions for the AI extractor (e.g. "always use AUD for currency")

extractionConfig.description: AI extraction field definitions (extractor/upload feeds only)

dayOfWeek.description: Day of week for weekly schedules (0 = Sunday, 6 = Saturday)

dayOfMonth.description: Day of month for monthly schedules

201.description: Feed created — returns the full feed document

422.description: Validation failed — sourceConfig, fieldMappings, or source×dataType combination is invalid

errors.description: Hard errors that blocked the request

warnings.description: Soft warnings — resolve before triggering a sync

post.summary: Trigger a sync for a feed
post.description:
Creates a sync job and queues it for execution. By default, transaction feeds use incremental (cursor-based) syncing. Pass startDate and endDate (YYYY-MM-DD) in the JSON body for a specific date range. Trade feeds always require a date range. Poll GET /feeds/:fid/jobs/:jid to monitor progress.

If a previous sync is already running (or its lock is stuck), this endpoint returns 409. Two recovery options: set `forceCancelActive: true` to cancel the active sync and start a fresh one, or DELETE /feeds/:fid/jobs/:jid to cancel the specific in-flight job. Pass `resetCursors: true` to clear cursors before fetching (full re-sync); pass `skipDedup: true` to bypass per-record dedup for backfills.

feedId.description: The feed this job belongs to

409.description: A sync job is already running for this feed (set forceCancelActive=true to override)

feedId.description: The feed this job belongs to
The feed this job belongs to

delete.description: Cancels a created or in-progress job: terminates the workflow, releases the feed lock so the next sync can start, and marks the job `cancelled`. Returns 409 if the job is already completed/failed/cancelled.

feedId.description: The feed this job belongs to

post.summary: Trigger a learn cycle for a feed
post.description: Immediately triggers the Memory enrichment learn phase for a feed, reading back destination records to update learned patterns. Bypasses the normal 30-second dedup guard. No-ops if no Memory enrichment is configured. Returns { triggered: true } when dispatched.

post.summary: Preview enrichment output for sample records
post.description: Run the full enrichment pipeline (Memory → Rules → Alerts) on up to 50 sample records without persisting anything. Returns each record with its original values, enriched values, and a list of changed fields. Also returns a per-enrichment match count summary. Useful for testing rule configs and seeing what Memory has learned before running a full sync.

get.description: List integrations connected to the current workspace. Pass `?scope=family` to include integrations from every portal under this workspace. Family scope requires admin+ role and is only valid on a top-level workspace. Provider auth tokens are stripped from all responses.

schema.description: Resource scope. `self` (default) returns this workspace only. `family` includes every portal under it; each row gains `workspaceId` + `portal: { id, name, icon } | null`. Admin+ only, top-level workspaces only.

parameters.description: Resource scope. `self` (default) returns this workspace only. `family` includes every portal under it; each row gains `workspaceId` + `portal: { id, name, icon } | null`. Admin+ only, top-level workspaces only.
parameters.name: scope

200.description: List of integrations (auth tokens excluded). With `scope=family`, each row additionally carries `workspaceId` and `portal`.

400.description: Invalid `scope` value, or `scope=family` was requested against a portal workspace

get.summary: List API keys for this workspace

200.description: Active API keys for this workspace (keyHash never returned)

keyPrefix.description: First 8 chars of the key for display (e.g. bsk_a3f5)

expiresInDays.description: Days until expiry; omit for no expiry

cdrAcknowledged.description: Required for workspaces with CDR (Australian) banks: the customer acknowledges CDR data may be read into their own systems via this key.

cdrWordingVersion.description: Disclosure wording version acknowledged (paired with cdrAcknowledged).

201.description: API key created. The plaintext key is shown once — store it immediately.
